schema_source: grande_finale.tex \label{conj:Q} (+ cor:anticode-cap, prop:prefix-rigidity, thm:head-flatness, cor:head-q, rem:head-does-not-close-q, thm:moment-q); packet-extension for experimental/data/certificates/frontier-adjacent/kb_mca_conjq_rung_audit_v1.json (adds a NEW sibling file; that JSON is NOT modified)
deliverable_name: conj:Q rung routes -- exact dead margins for the three cheap closure routes at the KB-MCA v13 raw row (1116047,1116048)
status: ROUTE-CUT / EXACT_MARGINS / PROVED-LOCAL(H1 as a bound, H2 as a ceiling) / AUDIT. Does NOT prove or refute conj:Q; does NOT move the frontier edge; does NOT alter any verdict of kb_mca_conjq_rung_audit_v1.json.

cross_refs.conj:Q: prefix flatness (grande_finale.tex \label{conj:Q}); this packet shows three cheap routes to it are all dead with exact margins, at the same 5-rung ladder kb_mca_conjq_rung_audit_v1.json already charges
cross_refs.cor:anticode-cap: the packing bound |Fib_w(z)| <= C(n,m-w)/C(m,w) (PROVED in grande_finale.tex, itself a corollary of prop:prefix-rigidity); H1 below is this bound specialized to the 5-rung ladder plus its exact margin computation -- not a new theorem
cross_refs.prop:prefix-rigidity: distinct fiber members have |M\M'| >= w+1 (PROVED); the source rigidity fact behind cor:anticode-cap
cross_refs.thm:head-flatness: proved head-depth prefix flatness at the Weil sqrt(p) scale (PROVED); H2 below is this theorem specialized (ell=0) and solved exactly for w_head per rung -- not a new theorem
cross_refs.cor:head-q: the solved head of Q, w<=21 at the deployed row (PROVED); H2's w_head(L_0)=21 matches this to the integer
cross_refs.rem:head-does-not-close-q: why Q remains open: one sqrt(p) loss per active power-sum direction, reaching only w=21-22 while the frontier needs w~67466 -- this packet's H2 ladder table is the per-rung quantification of exactly this remark
cross_refs.thm:moment-q: moment criterion R<=exp((A+B)/r); the moment-route section's r=ceil(w log2 p / Delta) is thm:moment-q specialized to the optimistic case log2(Gamma_r)~0
cross_refs.conj:BC: split-pencil census (\label{conj:BC}); the only live lever identified here (Sec 4 / live_targets below); not touched, not advanced
cross_refs.thm:asymptotic: asymptotic closure from Q, BC, SP in their e^{o(n)} forms (\label{thm:asymptotic}); conj:Q's own text states R_Q(n)=e^{o(n)} suffices asymptotically -- the softer target noted as still live (Sec 4 / live_targets below)
cross_refs.kb_mca_conjq_rung_audit_v1.json: the 5-rung ladder (n_s,m_s,w_s), the descent identity (D), and K_raw=4807520 are REUSED verbatim from this already-integrated, already-verified sibling packet; not recomputed independently for the ladder shape, only for the route margins below

target.K_raw_note: floor(B* p^w / binom(n,m)); reproduced here by independent big-int recompute (verifier gate i) and matching kb_mca_conjq_rung_audit_v1.json's target.K_raw to the integer
target.bar_note: the SAME ladder bar rho_s <= K_raw used at every rung (kb_mca_conjq_rung_audit_v1.json Sec 6); all margins below are measured against log2(K_raw), not the (numerically indistinguishable) budget_bits=22.196861708

h1_packing_bound.theorem: cor:anticode-cap (PROVED): |Fib_w(z)| <= C(n,m-w)/C(m,w) for every z, primitive or not; for the n=2k ladder rows this collapses exactly to rho(z) <= p^w / C(k-1,w)
h1_packing_bound.proof_sketch: prop:prefix-rigidity gives |M\M'|>=w+1 for distinct fiber members, i.e. |M cap M'| <= k=m-w-1; no (k+1)-subset lies in two fiber members (Johnson/Fisher/Deza-Frankl packing), giving |Fib_w(z)| <= C(n,k+1)/C(m,k+1) = C(n,m-w)/C(m,w); the n=2k algebra collapses this to p^w/C(k-1,w). Toy-verified exactly (verifier gate ii): rigidity holds, packing holds, and the n=2k clean-form identity C(k-1,w)*C(n,m-w) == C(n,m)*C(m,w) holds EXACTLY (Fraction/big-int, not approximately) on a toy row.

bits_per_depth.reading: packing sees only ~5.35 bits/depth of combinatorial (Hamming-distance) entropy (= (k/w)H(w/k), H(w/k)~0.344); the budget needs the full log2(p)=30.99 bits/depth; the ~25.64 bits/depth deficit is structural (Hamming geometry cannot see the field size p), not a slackness gap

consequences: Singleton / Johnson / RCW / Delsarte-with-distance-only bounds are all <= H1, hence all DEAD
Plotkin is inapplicable: needs d/n ~ 1/2, here d/n = 2(w+1)/n ~ 0.064 at every rung
primitivity gives no rescue: cor:anticode-cap already holds for every z (primitive or not), and the ~1e5-1e6 bit gap dwarfs any conceivable primitive discount
h1_packing_bound.verdict: DEAD at every rung: over budget by 1.73e6 bits (L_0) down to 1.08e5 bits (L_4)

h2_head_flatness_ceiling.theorem: thm:head-flatness at ell=0 (PROVED): nonvacuous exactly for w <= w_head(n,m) := floor((n-m)/ceil(sqrt p))
h2_head_flatness_ceiling.deployed_note: w_head(L_0) = 21 EXACTLY, matching cor:head-q's proved 'w<=21' at the deployed row to the integer
h2_head_flatness_ceiling.degradation_reading: n halves each rung but ceil(sqrt p) is fixed, so w_head halves in lockstep with w_needed; the ratio (gap_ratio) stays 3200x-4200x and never improves -- the shallowest rung (L_4) is in fact the WORST for head-flatness (w_head=1)
h2_head_flatness_ceiling.terminus_reading: the 2-adic symmetric descent hard-caps at s=4 (v2(m_safe)=4; L_4's m_4=69753 is ODD so it admits no further descent), where head-reach is w_head=1 against the needed w=4216 -- a gap that is EXACTLY 4216x at the terminus (not merely 'about'), and never improving at any rung
h2_head_flatness_ceiling.strategic_correction: this corrects the aspirational reading of cap25_v13_qfin_rung_audit.md Sec 6 ('halving TOWARD the proved head-depth base cases, rem:proved-q-part') and Sec 7 ('rungs step the depth w down by factors of two TOWARD the proved head-depth base cases'): the ladder's rungs are all equidistant (~3200x-4200x) from head-reach, not converging toward it; descending the ladder does not bring the primitive-flatness problem closer to the proved head-depth cases
h2_head_flatness_ceiling.verdict: DEAD at every rung: the required depth exceeds the head-flatness ceiling by 3200x-4200x, worst (4216x) at the terminus L_4

moment_route.theorem: thm:moment-q (PROVED): R <= exp((A+B)/r) where Gamma_r<=exp(A), w log|B|<=B; specialized here at the optimistic log2(Gamma_r)~0, giving r = ceil(w log2(p) / Delta) for a target budget Delta bits
moment_route.L_4_highlight: even at the most generous 34.793-bit standalone budget, the moment bridge needs r>=3756; at the shared ladder bar (22.197 bits), r>=5886. Head-flatness (H2) supplies only w<=1 there -- short by a factor of ~3750-5900 at the terminus
moment_route.verdict: DEAD at every rung: required moment order exceeds the provable head-depth order by 3 to 4 orders of magnitude

toy_validation.method: exhaustive fiber enumeration, independent reimplementation (not imported from any prior lane's script)

clean_form_identity_check.identity: C(k-1,w) * C(n,m-w) == C(n,m) * C(m,w)  (exact integer identity underlying rho<=p^w/C(k-1,w) at n=2k rows)

conjQ_route_margins.strategic_correction_summary: cap25_v13_qfin_rung_audit.md Sec 6 and Sec 7 read the ladder as stepping/halving depth 'toward the proved head-depth base cases'; H2 shows this is not so -- w_head shrinks in lockstep with w_needed down the ladder (21,10,5,2,1 against needed 67471,33735,16867,8433,4216), so every rung is equally (~3200x-4200x) far from head-reach, and L_4 (the shallowest, cheapest-looking rung) is in fact the worst case (w_head=1). The base does not scale with the rungs. This is a correction to how the existing ladder should be read, not a change to any of its proved facts, verdicts, or integers.

live_targets.conj_BC_split_pencil: L_4's row (n_4=131072, m_4=69753 ODD, w_4=4216) is structurally the cleanest primitive-flatness target for the conj:BC split-pencil / divisor-count route: m_4 odd means v2(m_4)=0, so every m_4-subset has subset-symmetry s=0 -- the ENTIRE fiber at L_4 is subset-primitive (no ladder/quotient correction term at all). Counting locator factorizations Lambda_M = G*A with fixed top-w coefficients via divisor bounds sees p directly, where Weil/Parseval cancellation (H2's barrier) does not.
live_targets.asymptotic_envelope: conj:Q's own statement records that R_Q(n)=e^{o(n)} (or even poly(n) with log agreement reserve) suffices for the asymptotic frontier (thm:asymptotic then closes the RS-MCA threshold from Q, BC, SP in their e^{o(n)} forms). This softer asymptotic target sidesteps the finite 2^22.197 bar entirely and is a live, different-in-kind target from the finite KB-MCA row bound this packet closes off.

non_claims: does not move the frontier edge (1116047,1116048)
does not alter any verdict, integer, or margin printed in kb_mca_conjq_rung_audit_v1.json or its companion note
does not touch conj:BC or conj:SP
the strategic correction concerns proof-ROUTE selection (which cheap bounds can close a rung) and how the existing ladder's rungs relate to head-reach, not the ledgers or verdicts themselves
